behaviors.description: Required patient fields are validated before downstream processing; missing fields return one error listing all missing items.
behaviors.justification: Failure is in module handoff/order (validator must gate pipeline), not just single-function rules. Integration is needed to prove downstream steps are not invoked when validation fails. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow: behavior text has no real OS/network/subprocess or AI-model-output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run intake orchestrator with payload missing multiple required fields; assert returned error contains all missing keys and assert insurance/triage/scheduling modules were not executed.

behaviors.description: Date of birth validation rejects future dates, dates older than 150 years, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: This is pure input-validation logic at a single function/object boundary; higher-level wiring adds no new reality signal for this specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven validator tests for valid DOB, future date, >150-year-old date, and malformed strings.

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility sends correct patient ID and plan code to payer API and interprets eligible/not eligible/pending.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on real HTTP boundary and external contract shape (request/response mapping). Mocked clients can hide real serialization/contract mismatches. System Triad applies (real external response contract). Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI-model judgment or multi-agentic chain in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real payer sandbox endpoint over HTTP, submit eligibility requests and assert outbound fields (patient ID, plan code) and correct mapping of real responses to eligible/not_eligible/pending.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, eligibility is marked pending and intake continues.
behaviors.justification: Unreachable-network behavior is real infra (timeouts/connection failures). In-process fakes can miss transport-layer failure modes; System required by REAL-INFRA.
behaviors.test_description: Induce real network unreachability to payer endpoint (e.g., blocked host or refused connection), then assert pipeline continues and eligibility state becomes pending.

behaviors.description: Triage invocation payload includes reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric.
behaviors.justification: This checks wiring/payload construction to AI client, not model output quality. Integration catches handoff bugs between config, symptom intake, and triage client. Rejected Agentic: behavior text only requires payload contents, not correctness of model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke triage service with known symptoms and rubric config using a deterministic stub AI endpoint; assert exact payload fields sent include symptoms and rubric.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage assigns high urgency for emergency symptom combinations (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model judgment/output quality; stubs would hide the failure. This is the deterministic-assertion trap case.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated live triage calls on emergency symptom cases and assert high-urgency classification rate meets threshold; track variance across runs.

behaviors.description: Triage parser stores a clinician-readable explanation field, not only numeric score.
behaviors.justification: This is parser-to-storage handoff across modules (parse + persistence), typically catchable without real model quality checks. Rejected Agentic: behavior text is about storing field presence/content slot, not whether explanation quality is clinically good.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative triage response fixture containing score and explanation through parse-and-persist path; assert persisted record includes non-empty explanation field.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage explanation cites case-specific symptoms and rationale, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: Behavior evaluates LLM output quality/relevance. Deterministic stubs cannot validate symptom-specific rationale quality.
behaviors.test_description: Submit multiple live symptom cases and score explanations for mention of case-specific symptoms plus explicit urgency rationale; fail if responses are generic boilerplate.

behaviors.description: Scheduler picks first available slot matching urgency SLA (high same-day, medium within 48h).
behaviors.justification: Requires coordination between urgency output, availability query, and scheduling logic with shared calendar DB. Local DB/module wiring is sufficient; no mandatory real external API/LLM dependency in this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Using seeded calendar DB state, run scheduling for high/medium urgency and assert selected slot is earliest one satisfying same-day/48h policy.

behaviors.description: Scheduling avoids double-booking when two patients are processed concurrently.
behaviors.justification: Race-condition risk emerges from concurrent access + DB constraints/transactions across modules. Integration with real DB concurrency is the minimum additional check. Rejected System: behavior text does not require subprocess/real external API; concurrency can be exercised in-process against DB.
behaviors.test_description: Launch two concurrent scheduling requests targeting same slot against test calendar DB and assert exactly one booking succeeds (or one is retried to next slot) with no duplicate row/state.

behaviors.description: Confirmation message includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and visit-type-appropriate prep instructions.
behaviors.justification: Primarily deterministic message composition/template logic; no extra reality signal from higher-level infra for this specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test confirmation formatter across visit types and assert required fields and prep text are present and correct.

behaviors.description: Pipeline orchestration (validate→insurance→triage→schedule→confirm) succeeds with deterministic stubbed triage output.
behaviors.justification: Explicitly an orchestration/state-handoff behavior with deterministic stubbed AI, so integration is the right brake point. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: behavior text explicitly says stubbed triage output (no real model judgment).
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end intake in-process with stubbed triage and stubbed external boundaries; assert each stage runs in order, passes expected state, and final confirmation is produced.

behaviors.description: For one real patient submission, full live pipeline completes without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Pipeline includes live AI triage call; execution path and downstream branching depend on real model output, so mock-based checks can give false confidence. Also touches real external infra, but Agentic is the tighter requirement due to model-dependent correctness in live run.
behaviors.test_description: Run one real submission through live validate/insurance/triage/schedule/confirm path and assert automatic completion plus produced appointment and confirmation artifacts.

behaviors.description: For a returning patient, full live pipeline uses broader context (history/cross-step state) so downstream decisions stay consistent with earlier triage/scheduling outputs without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This is multi-step, cross-stage consistency over a live pipeline with context carryover and LLM-involved decision handoff. MULTI-STEP + LLM-involved orchestration makes Workflow the minimum to avoid false confidence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live returning-patient intake with prior-history context enabled; verify triage decision, scheduling outcome, and later steps remain mutually consistent with carried context across the full pipeline.
